channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Empty tree bed, 16th April
item.description:
Empty tree bed opposite 42 Cromwell road hoping you could please check it’s on the planting request list thanks

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 6 x gas canister dumped at Petts Wood Memorial Hall, 16th April
item.description:
Left 6x gas canisters by entrance to garage area, found in Petts Wood Memorial Hall gardens

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Petts Wood Road, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Domestic rubbish fly tipped stretching about 15metres along the top of Alkham Road., 16th April
item.description:
Persistent spot for fly tipping. This is the second time this week so it just seems to be getting worse. Could this spot be monitored by CCTV so that the culprits be caught?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cazenove Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside The Rosewood Hotel, 16th April
item.description:
This BT cover has been broken for many weeks now. Please could you chase them down to get it fixed? Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 90, High Holborn, Holborn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More trash on pavement, 16th April
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ceres Road, Plumstead

item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kimberley Road, London

item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load (Estates)

item.title: Large pothole just before crossroads, 16th April
item.description:
Large pothole, unavoidable by drivers if cars coming in opposite direction, needs fixing

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sherwood Road, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: All the saplings in Kelly Street, NW1, 16th April
item.description:
The watering truck visited Kelly Street this morning but the men did not get out of the cab to do their job. When I got them to slow down and challenged them they claimed they had done it, but they had not. This is the second time the truck has visited our street but the men have not carried out their task. Kindly require them to come back and do their job properly. We will be checking daily.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 33, Kelly Street, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Athelstan gardens, 16th April
item.description:
Fly tipping

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kimberley Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load (Estates)

item.title: Black corner shelving unit, 16th April
item.description:
Black shelving unit dumped on grass outside the entrance to the bungalows.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rancliffe Gardens, Eltham

item.title: Littering and fly tipping, 16th April
item.description:
Excess littering and fly tipping on weekly basis please keep a eye on it

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Leabourne Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Thames water works causing severe disruption, 16th April
item.description:
I am writing to express my deep concern regarding the ongoing Thames Water works at the top of Kimberly Road, which are scheduled to continue until October. While I understand that these works may be necessary, the current traffic management arrangements are causing severe disruption and, more importantly, posing a serious safety risk to residents. The closure of access via Kimberly Drive has left our area with only one road in and out. This road is narrow and lined with parked cars, making it extremely difficult for traffic to flow. As a result, congestion has become unmanageable. On one occasion, it took me two and a half hours to return home due to the lack of alternative routes. More concerning, however, is the impact on emergency services. Earlier this week, my neighbour required an ambulance, and due to the congestion on the single access road, the ambulance was unable to reach her promptly. This is completely unacceptable. In the event of a fire or any other emergency requiring urgent response, there is a very real risk that services would be delayed or unable to gain access altogether. This situation presents a clear hazard to public safety. Residents should not be placed in a position where emergency assistance cannot reach them in a timely manner. I would like to understand: What risk assessments have been carried out regarding emergency access? Why no alternative access routes or traffic management solutions have been put in place? What immediate actions will be taken to ensure emergency vehicles can access the area without obstruction? I will also be raising this matter with the fire brigade, as I have been advised that this situation constitutes a significant safety concern. I urge the council to treat this matter with urgency and to work with Thames Water to implement a safer and more effective solution as soon as possible. I look forward to your prompt response.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Longmead Drive, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Streetlight for MONTHS, 16th April
item.description:
Reported many times. Have been told the light no longer has power, but not why no power been provided to what was a fully functioning street light.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 30, Wandsworth Road, Vauxhall, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping on alkham road, 16th April
item.description:
This is a regular fly tipping spot it needs clearing and cctv installing to prevent a reoccurrence

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cazenove Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown shrubs, 16th April
item.description:
The shrubs outside properties 173–176 are causing a safety hazard, as they obstruct residents’ visibility of oncoming traffic and prevent drivers from clearly seeing vehicles approaching from behind the shrubs.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 111-117, Asylum Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Vehicle, 16th April
item.description:
HK57 VWG - parked across driveway. Vehicle left unlocked, boot open and windows opened. Has tried reporting to police but not taking action. Vehicle been present since overnight. On Diameter Road between Crescent Driver and Rolleston Avenue.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Diameter Road, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road cracks, 16th April
item.description:
House shakes several times a minute Constantly reported in past Unbearable 12 years ongoing
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 263, Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Piles of glass in the road and public path, 16th April
item.description:
Smashed in car windows outside a house resulting in approx 3 piles of large shattered window glass in road which could damage tyres and on foot path dangerous!!!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bourne Vale, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Damage - smashed glass or panels / jagged metal edges on shelter
